goal: Compare the standard API input price per 1 million tokens (base/standard tier) for three frontier LLMs — Google Gemini 2.5 Pro, Anthropic Claude Opus 4.x, and OpenAI's current GPT-5 flagship — and determine which has the cheapest input price and the figure.
expected: Google Gemini 2.5 Pro has the cheapest input price at $1.25 per 1M tokens (prompts <=200k), roughly 4x cheaper than Claude Opus 4.8 ($5/1M) and OpenAI GPT-5.5 ($5/1M, tied), as of 2026-06-18.
answer:
# API Input Price Comparison: Gemini 2.5 Pro vs. Claude Opus 4.x vs. GPT-5

## Pricing Summary (Standard/Base Tier, per 1M Input Tokens)

| Model | Provider | Input Price (per 1M tokens) | Output Price (per 1M tokens) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Gemini 2.5 Pro** | Google | **$1.25** (≤200K ctx) / $2.50 (>200K ctx) | $10.00 |
| **Claude Opus 4.x** | Anthropic | **$5.00** (current versions: 4.5–4.8) | $25.00 |
| **GPT-5** | OpenAI | **$1.25** | $10.00 |

---

## Key Findings

### 🏆 Cheapest Input Price (Tie): **Google Gemini 2.5 Pro** & **OpenAI GPT-5** — both at **$1.25 / 1M input tokens**

- **Google Gemini 2.5 Pro**: $1.25/1M input tokens for prompts up to 200K tokens; rises to $2.50/1M for longer contexts. Output is $10.00/1M tokens [1][2][3].
- **OpenAI GPT-5**: $1.25/1M input tokens (standard tier), $10.00/1M output tokens. Released August 7, 2025; 400K context window [4][5].
- **Anthropic Claude Opus 4.x**: The most expensive of the three at **$5.00/1M input tokens** for current active versions (4.5, 4.6, 4.7, 4.8) and $25.00/1M output tokens. (Note: the original Claude Opus 4.0 and 4.1 were priced at $15/1M input but are now retired/deprecated.) [6]

---

## Notes & Caveats

1. **Claude Opus 4.x pricing has dropped significantly**: Early Opus 4 was $15/1M input tokens, but Anthropic cut prices substantially with versions 4.5 onward, now at $5/1M.
2. **Gemini 2.5 Pro has a tiered context price**: The $1.25 rate applies only to prompts ≤200K tokens. Longer prompts cost $2.50/1M input tokens [2][3].
3. **GPT-5 vs. GPT-5.x variants**: OpenAI's lineup has since expanded to GPT-5.4 ($2.50/1M) and GPT-5.5 ($5.00/1M), but the base **GPT-5** model remains at $1.25/1M input tokens [4][5].
4. One aggregator (pricepertoken.com) listed GPT-5 at $0.625/1M — this may reflect a cached/discounted rate or a data error; the majority of corroborating sources confirm **$1.25/1M** as the standard rate [4][5][7].

citations.title: Gemini 2.5 Pro Token Calculator: API Cost, 100K & 1M Tokens
citations.note: Confirms Gemini 2.5 Pro standard input price of $1.25/1M tokens (≤200K context), verified 2025-09-22, sourced from Google's official AI documentation.

citations.title: Billing Discrepancy for Gemini 2.5 Pro Usage – Google AI Developers Forum
citations.note: Google developer forum post referencing official Gemini 2.5 Pro input price of $1.25/1M tokens for prompts ≤200K tokens and $10/1M output tokens.

citations.title: Gemini 2.5 Pro – API Pricing & Benchmarks | OpenRouter
citations.note: Lists Gemini 2.5 Pro at $1.25/1M input tokens and $10/1M output tokens, 1,048,576 token context window.

citations.title: GPT-5 – API Pricing & Benchmarks | OpenRouter
citations.note: Confirms GPT-5 standard input price of $1.25/1M tokens, $10/1M output tokens, released August 7, 2025, 400K context window.

citations.title: LLM API Pricing Comparison 2025: GPT-5, Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek
citations.note: Corroborates GPT-5 at $1.25/1M input tokens and $10/1M output tokens as of November 2025.

citations.title: Pricing – Claude API Docs (Anthropic Official)
citations.note: Official Anthropic pricing page: Claude Opus 4.5–4.8 at $5/1M input tokens, $25/1M output tokens. Retired Opus 4.0 and deprecated 4.1 were $15/1M input.

citations.title: GPT-5 Mini: The Best AI Model for Price, Speed & Power
citations.note: States the flagship GPT-5 costs $1.25/1M input tokens and $10/1M output tokens, with GPT-5 Mini at $0.25/1M input.

confidence: medium — High confidence on Claude Opus 4.x ($5/1M) from Anthropic's own docs. Medium-high confidence on Gemini 2.5 Pro ($1.25/1M) from multiple corroborating sources referencing Google's official pricing page. Medium confidence on GPT-5 ($1.25/1M) from third-party aggregators (OpenRouter, OpenRouter corroborated by two articles); OpenAI's own pricing page was fetched but listed GPT-5.5/5.4 as current flagships without a plain "gpt-5" line visible, suggesting the model may have been superseded in their UI.

per_judge.reasoning: The answer contradicts the ground truth: it says Gemini 2.5 Pro ties with GPT-5 at $1.25/1M, while the correct comparison is against OpenAI's current flagship GPT-5.5 at $5/1M, making Gemini alone the cheapest at $1.25/1M. It is also not faithful to the retrieved evidence, which explicitly showed OpenAI's official pricing page listing GPT-5.5 at $5/1M and not GPT-5 as the current flagship; several extra claims about versions and retirements are unsupported.

The agent correctly priced Gemini 2.5 Pro ($1.25) and Claude Opus ($5.00), but failed to identify GPT-5.5 as the current flagship ($5.00). Consequently, it incorrectly claimed a tie for the cheapest model between Gemini 2.5 Pro and base GPT-5.

The agent correctly identifies Gemini 2.5 Pro at $1.25 and Claude Opus 4.x at $5, but the ground-truth treats GPT-5.5 ($5) as OpenAI's current flagship and names Gemini as the sole cheapest; the agent instead used the older base GPT-5 ($1.25) and declared a tie, which contradicts the ground-truth's single cheapest answer. The claims are well-grounded in retrieved evidence and properly cited.
